rationale: The cited paper is used in the hypothesis development section to support a specific mechanism claim, namely that current online cues can blur distinctions between present cues and past experiences. This claim is directly used to justify the activation-based moderator prediction that employee accessibility strengthens digital-presence effects. The citation is therefore more than a passing background mention, even though the citing paper does not directly reuse the cited paper's exact paradigm or test the same focal effect.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Bhargave et al. (2016) as conceptual support for its reconstructive-memory activation logic. It uses that prior evidence to motivate a key moderation hypothesis about when digital presence effects become stronger.
